description: Automated weather forecast trading system for Polymarket temperature markets, built with Elixir/Phoenix LiveView. Detects underpriced temperature shares at event creation, auto-buys using multi-model forecast consensus, and tracks positions with sell/hold recommendations.

userStories.title: Project scaffold and dependencies
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Phoenix project created with all dependencies and configuration so I can begin building features.
acceptanceCriteria: Phoenix 1.7+ project created with name 'weather_edge'
mix.exs includes dependencies: phoenix, phoenix_live_view, phoenix_live_dashboard, ecto_sql, postgrex, oban, req, jason, timex
mix.exs includes crypto dependencies: ex_secp256k1, ex_abi, ex_keccak
Oban configured in config.exs with queues: scanner(5), forecasts(3), trading(2), signals(3), cleanup(1)
Oban cron plugin configured with all 8 worker schedules as specified in the architecture
config/runtime.exs reads env vars: POLYMARKET_PRIVATE_KEY, POLYMARKET_API_KEY, POLYMARKET_API_SECRET, POLYMARKET_API_PASSPHRASE, POLYMARKET_WALLET_ADDRESS
config/runtime.exs sets chain_id: 137 (Polygon mainnet)
Trading safety config: min_reserve_usdc 2.0, max_orders_per_minute 6, max_position_per_event 1, order_retry_delay_ms 30000
Forecasts config: models list and base URLs for Open-Meteo and METAR
.env.example file with all required environment variables documented
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Docker Compose setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Docker Compose deployment so the system runs as a self-contained stack.
acceptanceCriteria: Dockerfile with multi-stage build (builder + runner) for the Elixir application
docker-compose.yml with two services: app (Elixir) and db (timescale/timescaledb:latest-pg16)
Database service exposes port 5432 with persistent volume for data
App service depends on db, loads .env file, exposes port 4000
App service runs migrations on startup before starting the server
docker-compose up builds and starts both services without errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Stations table migration and schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the stations table to store weather station configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration creates stations table with columns: id (bigserial PK), code (varchar(4) unique not null), city (varchar(100) not null), latitude (double precision not null), longitude (double precision not null), country (varchar(2) not null), wunderground_url (text), monitoring_enabled (boolean default true), auto_buy_enabled (boolean default false), max_buy_price (double precision default 0.20), buy_amount_usdc (double precision default 5.00), slug_pattern (varchar(200)), inserted_at (timestamptz), updated_at (timestamptz)
WeatherEdge.Stations.Station Ecto schema with all fields and proper types
Changeset validates required fields: code, city, latitude, longitude, country
Changeset validates code uniqueness and uppercase format
Migration runs successfully: mix ecto.migrate
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: MarketCluster table migration and schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the market_clusters table to store Polymarket temperature events.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration creates market_clusters table with columns: id (bigserial PK), event_id (varchar(100) unique not null), event_slug (varchar(200) not null), station_code (varchar(4) references stations(code)), target_date (date not null), title (varchar(300)), outcomes (jsonb not null), resolved (boolean default false), resolution_temp (integer), inserted_at (timestamptz), updated_at (timestamptz)
Index on (station_code, target_date)
Partial index on resolved WHERE resolved = false
WeatherEdge.Markets.MarketCluster Ecto schema with all fields
Schema belongs_to :station with foreign key on code
Migration runs successfully
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: MarketSnapshot hypertable migration and schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the market_snapshots TimescaleDB hypertable to store price history.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration creates market_snapshots table with columns: id (bigserial), market_cluster_id (bigint not null references market_clusters), snapshot_at (timestamptz not null), outcome_label (varchar(30) not null), yes_price (double precision), no_price (double precision), volume (double precision)
Composite primary key on (id, snapshot_at)
Migration executes SELECT create_hypertable('market_snapshots', 'snapshot_at')
WeatherEdge.Markets.MarketSnapshot Ecto schema with all fields
Schema belongs_to :market_cluster
Migration runs successfully against TimescaleDB
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: ForecastSnapshot hypertable migration and schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the forecast_snapshots TimescaleDB hypertable to store weather model predictions over time.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration creates forecast_snapshots table with columns: id (bigserial), station_code (varchar(4) not null references stations(code)), fetched_at (timestamptz not null), target_date (date not null), model (varchar(20) not null), max_temp_c (double precision not null), hourly_temps (jsonb)
Composite primary key on (id, fetched_at)
Migration executes SELECT create_hypertable('forecast_snapshots', 'fetched_at')
WeatherEdge.Forecasts.ForecastSnapshot Ecto schema with all fields
Schema belongs_to :station with foreign key on code
Migration runs successfully
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Positions and Orders tables migration and schemas
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the positions and orders tables to track trades and order history.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration creates positions table with columns: id (bigserial PK), station_code (varchar(4) not null references stations(code)), market_cluster_id (bigint not null references market_clusters), outcome_label (varchar(30) not null), side (varchar(3) not null), token_id (text not null), tokens (double precision not null), avg_buy_price (double precision not null), total_cost_usdc (double precision not null), current_price (double precision), unrealized_pnl (double precision), status (varchar(20) default 'open'), recommendation (varchar(50)), auto_bought (boolean default false), opened_at (timestamptz), closed_at (timestamptz), close_price (double precision), realized_pnl (double precision)
Partial index idx_positions_open on status WHERE status = 'open'
Index idx_positions_station on station_code

Migration creates orders table with columns: id (bigserial PK), position_id (bigint references positions), station_code (varchar(4) not null), order_id (varchar(100)), token_id (text not null), side (varchar(4) not null), price (double precision not null), size (double precision not null), usdc_amount (double precision not null), status (varchar(20) default 'pending'), auto_order (boolean default false), error_message (text), placed_at (timestamptz), filled_at (timestamptz)
Partial index idx_orders_pending on status WHERE status = 'pending'
WeatherEdge.Trading.Position and WeatherEdge.Trading.Order Ecto schemas with all fields and associations
Position has_many :orders, Order belongs_to :position
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Signals hypertable and ForecastAccuracy table migrations and schemas
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the signals hypertable and forecast_accuracy table for mispricing alerts and calibration data.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration creates signals table with columns: id (bigserial), station_code (varchar(4) not null), market_cluster_id (bigint not null), computed_at (timestamptz not null), outcome_label (varchar(30) not null), model_probability (double precision not null), market_price (double precision not null), edge (double precision not null), recommended_side (varchar(3) not null), alert_level (varchar(20))
Composite primary key on (id, computed_at)
Migration executes SELECT create_hypertable('signals', 'computed_at')

Migration creates forecast_accuracy table with columns: id (bigserial PK), station_code (varchar(4) not null references stations(code)), target_date (date not null), predicted_distribution (jsonb not null), actual_temp (integer not null), model_errors (jsonb not null), best_edge (double precision), auto_buy_outcome (varchar(20)), auto_buy_pnl (double precision), resolution_correct (boolean not null), inserted_at (timestamptz)
Unique constraint on (station_code, target_date) for forecast_accuracy
WeatherEdge.Signals.Signal and WeatherEdge.Calibration.Accuracy Ecto schemas
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Aviation Weather METAR client
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an HTTP client to validate ICAO codes and fetch current weather observations from the Aviation Weather API.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdge.Forecasts.MetarClient module using Req
validate_station/1 calls GET https://aviationweather.gov/api/data/metar?ids=CODE
On valid ICAO: returns {:ok, %{code, city, latitude, longitude, country}} parsed from METAR response
On invalid ICAO (empty response): returns {:error, :invalid_station}
get_current_conditions/1 fetches and parses current METAR observation (temperature, wind, humidity)
Handles API errors and timeouts with {:error, reason}
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Stations context (CRUD + validation)
userStories.description: As a trader, I want to add, update, and remove weather stations so the system knows which locations to monitor.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdge.Stations context module with functions: create_station/1, update_station/2, delete_station/1, list_stations/0, get_station/1, get_by_code/1
create_station/1 accepts ICAO code, validates via MetarClient, auto-resolves lat/lng/city/country
Auto-generates slug_pattern from city name (e.g., 'highest-temperature-in-sao-paulo-on-*')
Returns {:error, :invalid_station} if METAR validation fails
New stations default: monitoring_enabled=true, auto_buy_enabled=false, max_buy_price=0.20, buy_amount_usdc=5.00
update_station/2 allows toggling monitoring_enabled, auto_buy_enabled, and changing max_buy_price, buy_amount_usdc
Broadcasts PubSub events on create/update/delete
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Gamma API client
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an HTTP client to discover temperature events from Polymarket's Gamma API.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdge.Markets.GammaClient module using Req
get_events/1 fetches GET https://gamma-api.polymarket.com/events with query params (active, closed, etc.)
search_events/1 searches by query string
get_event_by_slug/1 fetches a specific event by slug
All functions return {:ok, data} or {:error, reason}
Handles API errors, timeouts, and rate limiting gracefully
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Event parser and Markets context
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to parse Gamma API event JSON into MarketCluster records and persist them.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdge.Markets.EventParser module with parse_event/1 function
Extracts from raw event JSON: event_id, slug, title, target_date, station_code
Extracts array of market outcomes: question, outcome_label, yes_price, no_price, clob_token_ids (YES and NO), volume, liquidity
Correctly identifies temperature degree from market question (e.g., '27 degrees' -> '27C')
Handles edge bucket outcomes ('26C or below', '34C or higher')
WeatherEdge.Markets context with create_market_cluster/1, get_active_clusters/0, get_clusters_for_station/1, mark_resolved/2
Stores parsed events as MarketCluster records with outcomes in JSONB
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Open-Meteo multi-model forecast client
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to fetch temperature forecasts from 5 weather models via Open-Meteo API.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdge.Forecasts.OpenMeteoClient module using Req
fetch_all_models/3 accepts latitude, longitude, forecast_days and fetches hourly temperature_2m for models: gfs, ecmwf_ifs, icon_global, jma, gem_global in a single API call
extract_daily_max/2 extracts daily maximum temperature from hourly data for a given target_date
Returns %{model_name => max_temp_float} map (e.g., %{'gfs' => 28.3, 'ecmwf_ifs' => 27.8})
Handles partial model failures (some models unavailable) by returning available data only
WeatherEdge.Forecasts context with store_snapshot/1 to persist ForecastSnapshot records
Handles API errors and timeouts with {:error, reason}
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Gaussian smoothing module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Gaussian kernel smoothing module to spread probability mass across adjacent temperature outcomes.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdge.Probability.Gaussian module
apply_kernel/2 takes a raw probability map %{temp => prob} and sigma value, returns smoothed distribution
Gaussian kernel formula correctly applied: weight = exp(-(x-mu)^2 / (2*sigma^2))
sigma/1 returns appropriate sigma based on days out: <=1 day -> 0.8, 2 days -> 1.2, 3+ days -> 1.8
Smoothing spreads probability to neighboring temperatures (e.g., 28C probability partially spreads to 27C and 29C)
Output distribution sums to 1.0 after normalization
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Probability engine
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the probability engine that combines multi-model forecasts into a probability distribution across temperature outcomes.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdge.Probability.Engine module with compute_distribution/2 accepting station and target_date
Fetches latest forecasts from all models for the station and target_date
Rounds each model's max temp to integer (Polymarket uses whole degrees)
Builds empirical distribution from model frequency counts
Applies Gaussian smoothing via WeatherEdge.Probability.Gaussian
Collapses distribution tails into edge buckets (e.g., '26C or below', '34C or higher') using configurable bounds
Normalizes final distribution to sum = 1.0
WeatherEdge.Probability.Distribution struct with fields: probabilities map, top_outcome/0, top_n/1, probability_for/1 helpers
For 5 models predicting [28, 28, 27, 28, 27]: returns ~60% for 28C before smoothing
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: CLOB API client (read-only)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an HTTP client to read prices and orderbook data from Polymarket's CLOB API.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdge.Trading.ClobClient module using Req
get_price/2 fetches GET https://clob.polymarket.com/price?token_id=TOKEN&side=SIDE, returns float price
get_orderbook/1 fetches GET https://clob.polymarket.com/book?token_id=TOKEN, returns %{bids: [...], asks: [...]} with price and size per level
get_market/1 fetches market info by condition_id
All public endpoints work without authentication
Handles API errors and timeouts with {:error, reason}
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Data API client (positions and balance)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an HTTP client to read wallet positions and USDC balance from Polymarket's Data API.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdge.Trading.DataClient module using Req
get_positions/1 fetches GET https://data-api.polymarket.com/positions?user=WALLET_ADDRESS
get_activity/1 fetches GET https://data-api.polymarket.com/activity?user=WALLET_ADDRESS
get_balance/1 fetches USDC balance for wallet address
Wallet address loaded from application config
All functions return {:ok, data} or {:error, reason}
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Signal detector and alerter
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the mispricing detection engine that compares forecast probabilities to market prices and generates alerts.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdge.Signals.Detector module with detect_mispricings/2 accepting a MarketCluster and probability Distribution
For each temperature outcome: calculates edge_yes = model_prob - market_yes_price and edge_no = (1 - model_prob) - market_no_price
Recommends the side (YES or NO) with greater positive edge
Generates signal with alert levels: edge >= 0.08 AND liquidity > $20 -> 'opportunity', edge >= 0.15 AND liquidity > $20 -> 'strong', edge >= 0.25 -> 'extreme'
Safe NO detection: model_prob < 0.05 AND market_no_price <= 0.92 -> alert_level 'safe_no'
Structural mispricing check: if |sum_of_all_yes_prices - 1.0| > 0.05, returns :structural_mispricing flag
WeatherEdge.Signals.Alerter module broadcasts signals via PubSub to 'station:CODE:signal' topic
Signals context stores Signal records in database
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: EIP-712 and HMAC authentication module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need pure Elixir authentication for Polymarket CLOB API to place and manage orders.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdge.Trading.Auth module
L1 auth: EIP-712 domain separator for Polymarket exchange contract on Polygon (chain_id 137)
L1 auth: sign_order/1 produces valid EIP-712 typed data signature using ex_secp256k1 and ex_abi
L1 auth: correctly hashes EIP-712 struct (typeHash + encodeData) per EIP-712 spec
L2 auth: sign_request/3 produces HMAC-SHA256 signature using api_secret for method + path + body
L2 auth: returns headers map with POLY-ADDRESS, POLY-SIGNATURE, POLY-TIMESTAMP, POLY-NONCE, POLY-API-KEY, POLY-PASSPHRASE
Credentials loaded from application config (never hardcoded)
Private key held only in memory, never logged
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: CLOB authenticated endpoints (order placement)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need authenticated CLOB API endpoints to place and cancel orders on Polymarket.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdge.Trading.ClobClient extended with authenticated functions
place_order/5 (token_id, side, price, size, type \\ 'GTC') sends POST /order with EIP-712 signed order + L2 auth headers
cancel_order/1 sends DELETE /order with L2 auth headers
get_open_orders/0 sends GET /orders with L2 auth headers
Order struct built compatible with Polymarket CTF exchange contract
Returns {:ok, order_response} or {:error, reason}
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Order manager with safety guards
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an order manager that orchestrates order lifecycle with safety checks.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdge.Trading.OrderManager module
place_buy_order/3 accepts station, market outcome, and amount
Validates USDC balance >= order_amount + $2 minimum reserve before placing
Rejects duplicate orders: checks no existing order for same event + station
Enforces max 1 auto-buy per event per station
Rate limiting: max 1 order per 10 seconds per station (uses simple timestamp tracking)
On successful placement: creates Order record with status 'pending', creates/updates Position record
On order fill detection: updates Order status to 'filled', updates Position with tokens and avg_buy_price
On failure: retries once after 30 seconds, then sets Order status to 'failed' with error_message
Broadcasts PubSub events on order placed, filled, failed
All orders logged with timestamps for audit trail
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Position tracker with sell/hold recommendations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a position tracker that calculates P&L and generates data-driven sell/hold recommendations.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdge.Trading.PositionTracker module
update_position/1 fetches current YES price from CLOB, calculates unrealized_pnl and unrealized_pnl_pct
generate_recommendation/1 implements the full recommendation logic:
- pnl >= 100% AND model_prob < 0.40 -> 'SELL NOW'
- pnl >= 75% AND days_until_resolution <= 1 -> 'CONSIDER SELLING'
- pnl >= 50% AND model_prob > 0.50 -> 'HOLD'
- pnl < 20% AND model_prob < 0.20 -> 'CUT LOSS'
- model_prob > 0.60 AND days_until_resolution == 0 -> 'HOLD TO RESOLUTION'
- default -> 'MONITORING'
Updates position record with current_price, unrealized_pnl, and recommendation
sell_position/1 places a sell order via OrderManager and updates position status to 'sold'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Event scanner worker
userStories.description: As a trader, I want the system to scan Polymarket every minute around 6:01 AM ET for new temperature events.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdge.Workers.EventScannerWorker implements Oban.Worker with queue: :scanner
Oban cron schedules: '55-59 5 * * *' and '0-20 6 * * *' (ET timezone)
For each station with monitoring_enabled=true: queries Gamma API, filters events by slug_pattern or city name in title
Detects new events not already in market_clusters table (checks by event_id)
Parses new events via EventParser, stores as MarketCluster records
Broadcasts {:new_event, station_code, event} via PubSub to 'station:CODE:new_event'
If station has auto_buy_enabled=true: enqueues AutoBuyerWorker with {station_code, event_id} args
Handles API failures without crashing: logs error and returns :ok (retries next cron tick)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Auto-buyer worker
userStories.description: As a trader, I want the system to automatically buy the most likely temperature outcome when a new event opens at a low price.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdge.Workers.AutoBuyerWorker implements Oban.Worker with queue: :trading
Receives {station_code, event_id} args from EventScanner
Fetches multi-model forecasts via OpenMeteoClient for station lat/lng + target_date
Runs Probability.Engine to determine most likely outcome
Fetches current YES price for the top outcome via ClobClient
If YES price <= station.max_buy_price: places buy order via OrderManager for station.buy_amount_usdc
If YES price > max_buy_price: logs skip reason, broadcasts :auto_buy_skipped to PubSub
Safety guards enforced: balance check, duplicate check, max 1 per event
Checks secondary opportunities: other temps with YES < max_buy_price AND model_prob > 20%, flags as alert (no auto-buy)
Broadcasts :auto_buy_executed with purchase details on success
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Forecast refresh worker
userStories.description: As a trader, I want forecasts refreshed every 15 minutes so probability calculations stay current.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdge.Workers.ForecastRefreshWorker implements Oban.Worker with queue: :forecasts
Oban cron: '*/15 * * * *'
Queries all stations that have active (unresolved) market clusters
For each station: fetches fresh multi-model forecasts from OpenMeteoClient
Stores forecast snapshots in forecast_snapshots hypertable via Forecasts context
Broadcasts forecast updates via PubSub to 'station:CODE:forecast_update'
Handles partial failures per station without blocking other stations
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Mispricing detection worker
userStories.description: As a trader, I want mispricing signals generated every 5 minutes comparing forecasts to market prices.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdge.Workers.MispricingWorker implements Oban.Worker with queue: :signals
Oban cron: '*/5 * * * *'
For each active market cluster: runs Probability.Engine to get distribution, fetches current prices from ClobClient
Passes cluster + distribution to Signals.Detector to compute edges and alert levels
Stores generated signals in signals hypertable
Broadcasts signals via Signals.Alerter to 'station:CODE:signal' PubSub topic
Handles failures per cluster without blocking other clusters
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Price snapshot worker
userStories.description: As a developer, I need periodic price snapshots stored in TimescaleDB for charts and P&L.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdge.Workers.PriceSnapshotWorker implements Oban.Worker with queue: :signals
Oban cron: '*/5 * * * *'
For each tracked market (open positions + active monitored clusters): fetches YES/NO prices from ClobClient
Stores MarketSnapshot records in market_snapshots hypertable with current timestamp
Handles API failures per market without blocking others
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Position monitor worker
userStories.description: As a trader, I want my positions monitored every 10 minutes with updated P&L and sell/hold recommendations.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdge.Workers.PositionMonitorWorker implements Oban.Worker with queue: :signals
Oban cron: '*/10 * * * *'
Queries all positions with status 'open'
For each position: calls PositionTracker.update_position/1 to refresh price and P&L
For each position: calls PositionTracker.generate_recommendation/1 to update sell/hold advice
Broadcasts position updates via PubSub to 'portfolio:position_update'
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Balance worker
userStories.description: As a trader, I want my USDC balance refreshed automatically every 5 minutes.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdge.Workers.BalanceWorker implements Oban.Worker with queue: :trading
Oban cron: '*/5 * * * *'
Fetches USDC balance from DataClient using configured wallet address
Broadcasts balance via PubSub to 'portfolio:balance_update' topic
Handles API errors gracefully (logs warning, does not crash)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Resolution worker
userStories.description: As a trader, I want resolved events processed daily so P&L is finalized and forecast accuracy is tracked.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdge.Workers.ResolutionWorker implements Oban.Worker with queue: :cleanup
Oban cron: '0 23 * * *' (daily at 11 PM ET)
Queries market_clusters where resolved=false and target_date < today
For each: fetches actual temperature from Weather Underground page for the station
Updates market_cluster: resolved=true, resolution_temp=actual_temp
Updates related positions: status to 'resolved_win' or 'resolved_loss', calculates realized_pnl
Handles Weather Underground page parsing (extracts max temperature from history page)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Calibration system (resolver + bias tracker)
userStories.description: As a trader, I want forecast accuracy tracked per station and model so I can evaluate prediction quality.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdge.Calibration.Resolver module with process_resolution/2 accepting market_cluster and actual_temp
Compares predicted distribution (from latest forecast) vs actual temperature
Calculates per-model absolute error from forecast_snapshots for that station+date
Stores ForecastAccuracy record with predicted_distribution, actual_temp, model_errors JSONB, resolution_correct boolean
Tracks auto_buy_outcome ('win', 'loss', 'sold_early') and auto_buy_pnl
WeatherEdge.Calibration.BiasTracker module with stats_for_station/1 and stats_for_model/2
BiasTracker computes: mean error, MAE, hit rate (top prediction matched actual) per station per model
Called by ResolutionWorker after each event resolution
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: PubSub topic helpers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a centralized PubSub helper module defining all topics and broadcast/subscribe functions.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdge.PubSubHelper module (or similar name)

Topic functions: station_new_event(code), station_forecast_update(code), station_price_update(code), station_auto_buy(code), station_signal(code), portfolio_balance_update(), portfolio_position_update()
broadcast/2 wraps Phoenix.PubSub.broadcast with the app's PubSub name
subscribe/1 wraps Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe
All workers and contexts use these helpers instead of raw PubSub calls
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Router and dashboard LiveView skeleton
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Phoenix router and a basic dashboard LiveView that loads stations and subscribes to PubSub.
acceptanceCriteria: Router has live '/', DashboardLive (main dashboard)
Router has live '/stations/:code/events/:event_id', StationDetailLive (detail view)
WeatherEdgeWeb.DashboardLive mounts and loads all stations with their active market clusters and open positions
DashboardLive subscribes to all relevant PubSub topics on mount
DashboardLive renders a basic layout with placeholder slots for: header, station cards, signal feed, portfolio summary
Page loads without errors at http://localhost:4000
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Header component
userStories.description: As a trader, I want a dashboard header showing my balance, wallet address, and an Add Station button.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdgeWeb.Components.HeaderComponent function component
Displays 'WEATHER EDGE' title
Shows USDC balance (e.g., '$47.23 USDC')
Shows truncated wallet address (e.g., '0x1a2b...3c4d')
'+ Add Station' button triggers phx-click event handled by DashboardLive
Settings link/button present
Updates balance in real-time when portfolio:balance_update PubSub message received

Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add station modal component
userStories.description: As a trader, I want to add stations via a modal dialog by typing an ICAO code.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdgeWeb.Components.AddStationModalComponent function component
Modal opens when triggered by header 'Add Station' button
Text input for ICAO code: auto-uppercases, max 4 characters
Submit button triggers validation via Stations.create_station/1
Shows loading spinner during METAR API validation
On valid: displays resolved station info (city, country, coordinates) with confirm button
On invalid: shows error message 'Invalid ICAO code'
On confirm: creates station, closes modal, new station card appears in dashboard list
Modal can be closed with X button or clicking outside

userStories.title: Station card component
userStories.description: As a trader, I want each station displayed as a card with monitoring toggles and nested event cards.
acceptanceCriteria: WeatherEdgeWeb.Components.StationCardComponent function component
Shows station code and city name as card title
Toggle switches for 'Monitoring ON/OFF' and 'Auto-Buy ON/OFF' with phx-click handlers
Displays current max_buy_price and buy_amount_usdc values (editable inline or via small inputs)
Shows USDC balance
Shows next expected event opening time text
Lists tracked event dates
Renders EventCardComponent for each active event belonging to this station

Tests sigma values: 0.8 for 1 day, 1.2 for 2 days, 1.8 for 3 days
Tests tail collapsing into edge buckets

Tests structural mispricing: sum of YES prices = 1.08 -> flagged
